title: GST Registration Online

hero.headline: Fast & Reliable GST Registration with Professionals Law
hero.subtext: GSTIN in 5–7 days. Trusted by startups, SMEs & e-commerce sellers.

mainHeading: GST Registration Online in India – Apply for GST Certificate Easily

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: Goods and Services Tax (GST) is a single indirect tax applied on the supply of goods and services throughout India. Enacted under the GST Act 2017, it replaced various state and central taxes and implemented uniform tax system. GST Registration is compulsory for entities over the prescribed turnover limits special interstate and e-commerce businesses. Professionals Law provide complete assistance for GST Registration within the country, ensuring seamless compliance with all legal provisions under the GST regime.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: GST Registration is legally required for businesses crossing the specified turnover threshold or involved in certain categories of trade. It enables businesses to collect tax from customers and claim Input Tax Credit (ITC). Professionals Law assists entities and businesses to properly and expediently register in order to mitigate risks of non-compliance, penalties and loss of tax benefits.

criteria: Businesses with annual aggregate turnover exceeding:
₹40 lakhs for goods (₹20 lakhs in special category states)
₹20 lakhs for services (₹10 lakhs in special category states)
Businesses registered under previous tax laws (VAT, Excise, Service Tax)
Casual taxable persons or non-resident taxable persons

body.title: Legal Recognition and Compliance
body.description: GST registration legally recognizes a business under the tax system and avoids legal complications or penalties related to tax evasion.

body.title: Input Tax Credit (ITC) Eligibility
body.description: Registered businesses can claim input tax credit on purchases, reducing the overall cost of goods or services.

body.title: Facilitates Interstate Sales
body.description: With GST registration, businesses can sell goods and services across states without restrictions or additional taxes.

body.title: Improves Credibility with Buyers
body.description: GSTIN on invoices increases transparency and builds trust with customers, vendors and corporate clients.

body.title: Mandatory for E-commerce Sellers
body.description: GST registration is essential to sell on online platforms like Amazon, Flipkart and others.

documents: KYC Documents of the applicant (Email id, Mobile No. PAN, Aadhaar, Voter id, Bank Statement etc.)
Business address proof (utility bill, rent agreement, etc.)
Bank account details (cancelled cheque or bank statement)

body.description: Professionals Law assesses the business model and turnover threshold for applicability.
We collect, verify and organize the necessary documents.
Professionals Law submits the GST REG-01 form online on the GST portal with proper classification of business.
An Application Reference Number (ARN) is generated for tracking purposes.
If required, additional documents or clarification are provided. On successful verification, the GSTIN is issued.
The GST certificate is downloaded and shared with the client for display and use in invoicing.

body.title: GSTIN Approval
body.description: 3 to 7 working days (subject to portal response and additional queries)

body.title: ₹10,000 or 10% of the tax due (whichever is higher) for non-registration despite liability
₹100/day (up to ₹5,000) for delayed filing of GST returns
Seizure of goods and prosecution in case of fraudulent activities
Loss of Input Tax Credit if GST is not charged properly

body.question: Is GST registration mandatory for all businesses?
body.answer: No, only businesses crossing the threshold turnover or involved in specific activities like interstate supply or e-commerce need to register.

body.question: Can I voluntarily apply for GST registration?
body.answer: Yes, businesses can apply voluntarily even if are below the threshold, especially to claim ITC and gain credibility.

body.question: Is a separate GST registration required for each state?
body.answer: Yes, businesses operating in multiple states need to register separately in each state.

body.question: What is a GSTIN?
body.answer: GSTIN is a unique 15-digit identification number assigned upon successful GST registration.

body.question: Can I use one GST registration for all branches?
body.answer: If branches are within the same state and under the same PAN, they can be included under one registration. Otherwise, multiple registrations are required.

body.question: What are composite and regular GST schemes?
body.answer: The regular scheme allows ITC claims and requires monthly filings. The composition scheme offers lower tax rates but restricts ITC and is suitable for small businesses.

body.question: Is digital signature mandatory?
body.answer: Yes, for companies and LLPs, a digital signature is mandatory while filing the application.

body.question: What happens if I delay registration beyond the threshold date?
body.answer: You may face penalties and any GST collected during the period may become unrecoverable without proper registration.

body.question: Can Professionals Law help with GST amendments and return filings?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law offers comprehensive GST services including registration, amendment, return filing, audits and compliance management.
